pi_items.source: Cross-referenced setup_task.sh (line 9: rm -f /home/ga/Documents/fifteen_solved.png), export_result.sh (line 14: EVIDENCE_PATH="/home/ga/Documents/fifteen_solved.png"), and task description. Path is consistent across all task files.

Verified from GCompris-qt source code at https://github.com/gcompris/GCompris-qt/blob/master/src/activities/fifteen/ActivityInfo.qml — the title field is 'The fifteen game'. Also confirmed by source code directory name 'fifteen'.

Verified from GCompris-qt source code at fifteen.js: scramble function uses (items.currentLevel + 2) badly placed tiles, so Level 3 = 5 badly placed tiles. Level 3 is odd-numbered, so tile numbers ARE displayed (odd levels show numbers, even levels hide them per Fifteen.qml: bar.level % 2 == 1). There are 14 total levels. Level 3 uses the Fishing_Boat_Scene.svg background (levels 1-4).

Verified from GCompris-qt source code: Fifteen.qml has 'columns: 4' in Grid component; fifteen.js uses modulo 4 operations for position calculations and loops from 0 to 15 (16 cells = 4x4). Grid size is FIXED at 4x4 across ALL 14 levels — only scramble difficulty changes per level.

Verified from fifteen.js checkAnswer() function: 'if(items.model.get(i).value !== i + 1)' for i=0..14, meaning positions 0-14 must contain values 1-15 respectively. Position 15 (bottom-right) contains value 0 (empty space). Row-major order: row1=[1,2,3,4], row2=[5,6,7,8], row3=[9,10,11,12], row4=[13,14,15,blank].

Verified from ActivityInfo.qml: category field is 'discovery | logic'. In GCompris, the pipe separates main category ('discovery') from subcategory ('logic'). The 'Logic' tab appears within the Discovery/Computer Discovery section of the GCompris main menu.

Verified from GCompris documentation and Wikipedia: the discovery category uses a penguin (Tux) icon on the main menu. ActivityInfo.qml confirms the activity belongs to the 'discovery' main category. GCompris manual confirms Tux the penguin represents the discovery/explore category.

pi_items.metadata_value: Main Menu > Computer Discovery (penguin) > Logic tab > scroll down > The fifteen game
pi_items.verified_value: Main Menu > Computer Discovery (penguin) > Logic tab/filter > The fifteen game
pi_items.source: Verified from ActivityInfo.qml category 'discovery | logic' mapping to Computer Discovery > Logic in the UI. Whether scrolling is needed depends on the number of activities in the Logic subcategory and screen resolution. The activity exists in this category path per the source code metadata.

privileged_info_summary: The fifteen game is a sliding tile puzzle activity in GCompris located under Computer Discovery (penguin icon) > Logic subcategory. The game uses a FIXED 4x4 grid across all 14 levels. The agent must advance to Level 3, which has 5 badly-placed tiles and displays numbers on tiles (odd levels show numbers, even levels hide them). The background image for levels 1-4 is Fishing_Boat_Scene.svg. The solved state requires tiles 1-15 in row-major order: row1=[1,2,3,4], row2=[5,6,7,8], row3=[9,10,11,12], row4=[13,14,15] with the blank space at position 16 (bottom-right corner). The win condition triggers a flower bonus animation. Tiles can be moved by clicking a tile adjacent to the empty space, or by using arrow keys. The scramble algorithm only uses legal moves (adjacent swaps with empty space) so all generated puzzles are solvable. The agent must save a screenshot to /home/ga/Documents/fifteen_solved.png showing the solved state or congratulations animation.
